source.method: sphinx-gallery site + examples/ folder listing; per-recipe pages fetched via WebFetch for codes and physics

ground_truth.note: The cookbook is an ML-interatomic-potential (MLIP) + statistical-sampling collection. Its DFT/DFPT and lattice-dynamics overlap with the map is thin (one BTE recipe on kALDo, one phonon-UQ recipe on phonopy). Most of its physics is in nuclear quantum effects, enhanced sampling / free energy, and ML model quantities, which the map barely touches. This is where the real gap concentration is.

status_legend.covered: produced quantity or code already has a map node / rail
status_legend.gap-code: code the cookbook drives that is absent from the 27 rails
status_legend.gap-node: quantity the cookbook computes that has no map node
status_legend.gap-edge: producing method the map lacks (a new operator, not a new node)

axis_a_codes.what: Universal Python force-server for advanced MD: path-integral MD (PIMD), ring-polymer contraction, multiple time stepping, thermostats (PIGLET/GLE), instanton rate theory, replica exchange. Talks to force engines (LAMMPS, CP2K, metatomic models) over a socket.
produces: quantum heat capacity via scaled-coordinates estimator

credits.citation: V. Kapil et al. / M. Litman et al., i-PI 3.0: a flexible and efficient framework for advanced atomistic simulations, J. Chem. Phys. 161, 062504 (2024)
credits.license: MIT (GPL components); confirm from repo LICENSE

axis_a_codes.recommendation: RAIL. Highest-value new code. i-PI is a Trajectory producer (PIMD as a producer variant of the existing Trajectory node) and the natural home for the whole nuclear-quantum-effects layer (quantum kinetic energy, quantum heat capacity, isotope estimators). Broad field use. It also grounds the existing HeatCapacity node with a genuinely-quantum MD estimator, complementary to the map's harmonic HeatCapacity.

axis_a_codes.what: Enhanced-sampling and free-energy plugin: metadynamics, adaptive biasing, collective variables (coordination numbers, SOAP-based order parameters, distances). Patches into MD engines and i-PI. sum_hills reconstructs the free-energy surface.

credits.citation: G. A. Tribello, M. Bonomi, D. Branduardi, C. Camilloni, G. Bussi, PLUMED 2: New feathers for an old bird, Comput. Phys. Commun. 185, 604 (2014)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: RAIL. The producer for the biggest missing physics tier (free energy / PMF / collective variables). Pairs with a new FreeEnergy-tier node family. Very broad field use across MD/biomolecular/materials communities.

recipes: nearly all ML recipes: pet-mad, pet-mad-dos, ml-density, polarizability, learn-tensors-with-mcov, metatomic-plumed, ml-mm, water-ir-spectrum, pet-phonons, shiftml
axis_a_codes.what: Self-describing sparse-tensor data format (TensorMap) and the metatomic model interface for interoperable atomistic ML; TorchScript export lets a trained model drive LAMMPS/i-PI/GROMACS/ASE/PLUMED. Infrastructure, not a physics producer per se.
produces: the transport format for equivariant ML predictions (energies, forces, dipoles, polarizability, DOS, NMR shieldings)

credits.citation: F. Bigi, J. W. Abbott, P. Loche, et al., Metatensor and Metatomic: Foundational Libraries for Interoperable Atomistic Machine Learning, J. Chem. Phys. 164, 064113 (2026)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: REPRESENTATION-ONLY (grounding, not a rail). metatomic is the calculator interface a model attaches to; the map already treats the Potential leaf as provided. When PET-family models are encoded they enter via a metatomic-backed ASE/LAMMPS calculator; the citation is owed but it is not its own physics rail. Track as the standard MLIP-delivery mechanism.

axis_a_codes.what: DFT / Gaussian-and-plane-waves electronic-structure and AIMD package. In the cookbook used to batch-generate reference energies/forces (training-data generation).

credits.citation: T. D. Kuhne, M. Iannuzzi, M. Del Ben, et al., CP2K: An electronic structure and molecular dynamics software package, J. Chem. Phys. 152, 194103 (2020)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: RAIL (medium priority). A second first-principles engine alongside QE/VASP; grounds the same DFT ground-state nodes (TotalEnergy, Forces, Stress, Structure). Broad field use. The DFT nodes already exist, so it is a rail-grounding task, not a new-node task.

axis_a_codes.what: High-performance classical/biomolecular MD engine. In the cookbook: ML/MM hybrid simulations and water in a pulsed electric field.

credits.citation: M. J. Abraham, T. Murtola, R. Schulz, et al., GROMACS: High performance molecular simulations through multi-level parallelism from laptops to supercomputers, SoftwareX 1-2, 19 (2015)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: RAIL (medium-low priority). A Trajectory/MD producer variant like LAMMPS/GPUMD/OpenMM; the map already has these MD nodes. Value is mainly biomolecular/soft-matter reach, which is outside the map's current materials focus. Grounds Trajectory, Temperature, Pressure.

axis_a_codes.what: Long-timescale / saddle-search engine: nudged elastic band (NEB, climbing-image, OCI-NEB), dimer method, adaptive kinetic Monte Carlo. C++ client driven by an ASE/metatomic potential.

credits.citation: S. T. Chill, M. Welborn, R. Terrell, et al., EON: client-server software for long-time simulations of atomic scale systems, Model. Simul. Mater. Sci. Eng. 22, 055002 (2014)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: RAIL (medium priority). Direct independent confirmation of the deferred NEB-barrier hook: eOn is a second producer for the existing ReactionBarrier[construction=neb_mep] node, and adds saddle-point/dimer methods. Also a natural producer for the instanton-rate node (below).

axis_a_codes.what: Differentiable particle-mesh Ewald library (PyTorch) for long-range electrostatics inside ML potentials and long-distance-equivariant features.
produces: long-range electrostatic energy and forces (a contribution to the Potential/energy, not a standalone observable)

credits.citation: P. Loche et al., Fast and flexible range-separated models for atomistic machine learning (torch-pme), (2024)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: OUT-OF-SCOPE as a rail (representation-only). It computes an energy contribution internal to a potential; it produces no map-tier observable of its own. Cite if a range-separated potential is ever encoded, but do not mint a rail.

axis_a_codes.what: Interactive structure-and-property visualizer for the browser. Pure visualization; computes no physics.

credits.citation: G. Fraux, R. K. Cersonsky, M. Ceriotti, Chemiscope: interactive structure-property explorer for materials and molecules, J. Open Source Softw. 5, 2117 (2020)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: OUT-OF-SCOPE (pure visualization). No producer, no node. Noted for completeness only.

axis_a_codes.what: scikit-learn-compatible ML utilities for atomistic data: FPS/CUR sample and feature selection, PCovR, directional convex hull.
produces: dataset-selection indices, PCA/PCovR embeddings, GCH hull distances (ML-descriptor / data-analysis quantities, not physical observables)

credits.citation: A. Goscinski, V. P. Principe, G. Fraux, et al., scikit-matter: A Suite of Generalisable Machine Learning Methods Born out of Chemistry and Materials Science, Open Res. Europe 1, 81 (2021)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: OUT-OF-SCOPE (data-science tooling). Its outputs are model/data-analysis artifacts, not physical values the map records. Skip.

axis_a_codes.what: Descriptor engine: SOAP power spectra, lambda-SOAP equivariant features, LODE long-range features. The featurizer feeding linear/equivariant ML models.
produces: ML structural descriptors (SOAP, lambda-SOAP) - representations, not physical observables

credits.citation: featomic (formerly rascaline), lab-cosmo; cite alongside metatensor J. Chem. Phys. 164, 064113 (2026)

axis_a_codes.recommendation: OUT-OF-SCOPE (representation-only). Descriptors are model internals. Not a map node. Skip unless the map ever adds an explicit ML-descriptor tier (not currently in scope).

axis_a_codes.what: Anharmonic lattice-dynamics / BTE phonon-transport package. ALREADY a rail (kaldo).
axis_a_codes.recommendation: N/A - covered. The cookbook drives kALDo with a PET-MAD potential; every quantity in that recipe (dispersions, DOS, mode c, linewidths, mfp, kappa tensor) is already a map node. Independent confirmation that the thermal-transport DAG is well targeted.

axis_a_codes.what: Force engine (LAMMPS) and structure/driver toolkit (ASE). Both are existing rails.
produces: Trajectory, Potential, Structure, Temperature, Pressure, MSD, VACF (all existing nodes)

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Free energy as a function of one or more collective variables, F(s) = -kT ln P(s), reconstructed from a metadynamics bias (plumed sum_hills). The central observable of enhanced-sampling.
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: energy (per mole or per particle); axis is CV-dependent
axis_b_physics_nodes.spectrum_or_scalar: SPECTRUM-LAYER (function-valued: F versus a collective-variable axis). Fits the map's existing spectrum contract (axis + values), like PhononDOS.
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: Distinct from HelmholtzFreeEnergy (that node is the harmonic vibrational free energy from phonon frequencies; this is a configurational free-energy profile along a reaction/order coordinate). Distinct from QHAGibbsEnergy. No existing node is a function of a collective variable. Genuinely new.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: A low-dimensional order parameter of the configuration (O-O distance, coordination number, SOAP-based Steinhardt-like Q4/Q6). The coordinate the free-energy surface is projected onto.
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: varies (length, dimensionless count, or dimensionless order parameter)
axis_b_physics_nodes.spectrum_or_scalar: TIME-SERIES / source axis. Behaves like a derived coordinate, best modeled as a source/parameter node that the FreeEnergySurface node depends on.
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: No existing node. Not a physical observable in the evidence sense but a necessary axis definition for FreeEnergySurface. Consider representation-only (an axis descriptor) rather than an evidence node.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Nuclear quantum kinetic energy from PIMD via the centroid-virial or thermodynamic estimator; its anisotropy (kinetic-energy tensor) probes quantum effects on bonds. Directly gives the quantum correction absent from classical MD.
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: energy (eV or per particle); tensor variant has 3x3 directional structure
axis_b_physics_nodes.spectrum_or_scalar: SCALAR (plus an optional 3x3 tensor variant, like the kinetic-energy tensor).
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: Distinct from InternalEnergy (that is the harmonic-oscillator internal energy per mode from Bose-Einstein occupation; this is a PIMD ensemble estimator of nuclear KE). Distinct from any MD kinetic energy (classical equipartition). New.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Constant-volume heat capacity of a liquid/anharmonic system from PIMD via the scaled-coordinates estimator, C_V = k_B beta^2 (<eps_v^2> - <eps_v>^2 - <eps_v'>). Captures quantum suppression (water ~15 k_B/molecule).
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: energy per temperature (or k_B per molecule)
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: Overlaps the existing HeatCapacity node in QUANTITY but not in METHOD: HeatCapacity in the map is the harmonic per-mode c from phonon frequencies (crystalline). This is a fluctuation estimator from PIMD valid for liquids/strongly-anharmonic systems. Best as a HeatCapacity producer variant (new edge onto an existing node) rather than a new node, unless the map wants method-tagged siblings like it does for ThermalConductivity[transport_model=...]. Recommend a method-tagged producer.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Frequency-dependent infrared absorption n(w)alpha(w) from the power spectrum of the cell dipole time series (fluctuation-dissipation): n(w)alpha(w) = w^2 S_mu(w)/(6 V eps0 kB T c). Peaks governed by dipole derivatives (Born charges).
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: absorption coefficient (per length) versus angular frequency
axis_b_physics_nodes.spectrum_or_scalar: SPECTRUM-LAYER (absorption versus frequency axis). Fits the spectrum contract cleanly, like PhononDOS.
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: No existing spectral node. Distinct from PhononDOS (density of states, not an optical response). New. Confirms the deferred spectrum-layer hook for an optical spectrum.
producers: MD dipole autocorrelation (LAMMPS/i-PI + ML dipole model via metatrain)

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Cell dipole moment time series and its derivative (dynamical / Born effective charges) driving IR intensities; also the response variable in the pulsed-field dielectric recipe.
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: electric dipole moment (e*angstrom) as a per-frame vector; derivative is dimensionless (Born charge)
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: The map has BornCharges (a static/DFPT Z* tensor, a Sources leaf). The dynamical dipole time series is a different object (MD trajectory observable) that feeds IR. Related but not a merge: BornCharges is provided input; DipoleMoment(t) is a produced trajectory observable. Consider a Molecular-domain node.
producers: ML dipole model along MD (metatrain dipole head)

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Molecular polarizability tensor alpha (response to an external electric field), decomposed into lambda=0 (isotropic) and lambda=2 (anisotropic) spherical components. Basis of Raman intensities.
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: The map has DielectricTensor (bulk eps_infinity, a Sources leaf for the NAC correction) and StaticDielectricTensor. Molecular polarizability is the per-molecule analog and a distinct object (finite systems, Raman). Not a merge with the bulk dielectric tensor. New Molecular-domain node.
producers: equivariant ML model (featomic lambda-SOAP + linear regression)

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Per-atom NMR shielding tensor (3x3) and its isotropic part (chemical shift), with ensemble uncertainty. Used for structure determination (matching predicted to measured shifts).
axis_b_physics_nodes.spectrum_or_scalar: SCALAR per atom (isotropic) plus a 3x3 tensor (anisotropic, visualized as ellipsoids).
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: No existing node touches NMR. Entirely new physics domain (magnetic-resonance / spectroscopy). No merge risk.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Electronic density of states g(E) on an energy grid, with derived Fermi level and band gap. Note the CNN-integrated bandgap head.
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: states per energy per volume (or per cell) versus energy
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: CRITICAL false-merge risk with PhononDOS: both are 'DOS'. They are physically distinct (electronic states vs vibrational modes; energy axis in eV vs frequency axis in THz). Must be a separate node, not a PhononDOS producer. The map has BandGap already (a DFT ground-state scalar); ElectronicDOS is the function-valued parent that a gap can be read from. New spectrum node in the DFT/electronic domain.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: The real-space electron density rho(r) as a learned/derived field, and properties derived from it.
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: electrons per volume, on a spatial grid
axis_b_physics_nodes.spectrum_or_scalar: FIELD (3D grid) - beyond the current scalar+spectrum contract.
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: The QE scan already flagged scf-charge-density as a hidden new-node candidate waiting on the kernel. The cookbook independently confirms electron density as a target. No existing node. Likely representation-only until the map supports field-valued evidence.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Chemical reaction rate constant including nuclear quantum tunneling, from ring-polymer instanton theory (semiclassical). Complements classical transition-state theory rates.
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: inverse time (rate); or a dimensionless tunneling enhancement factor
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: The map has ActivationEnergy and ReactionBarrier[construction=neb_mep] (barrier heights, energies). A rate CONSTANT with tunneling is a different quantity (kinetics, not the static barrier). Not a merge. New kinetics node.

axis_b_physics_nodes.physics: Predictive uncertainty of an ML potential/model: committee/ensemble spread, last-layer prediction rigidity (LLPR), shallow-ensemble error bars. Propagated onto physical predictions (e.g. error bands on phonon dispersions).
axis_b_physics_nodes.dimension: same units as the predicted quantity (an error bar), or dimensionless rigidity
axis_b_physics_nodes.spectrum_or_scalar: META (an uncertainty attached to any prediction, not a physical observable of the material).
axis_b_physics_nodes.false_merge_check: No existing node. This is not a material property but a property of the MODEL. The map records physical evidence; committee disagreement is metadata about a prediction's reliability. Recommend it does NOT become an evidence node; if surfaced at all it belongs as an uncertainty attribute on records (the instance schema already has an 'uncertainty' field) rather than a node. Flag as out-of-scope-as-node.

axis_c_edges_methods.method: PIMD (path-integral molecular dynamics) as a Trajectory producer
axis_c_edges_methods.physics: Ring-polymer sampling of the quantum partition function: a Trajectory producer variant that additionally exposes quantum estimators. New operator producing Trajectory (and the quantum-estimator nodes) from Potential + Temperature.
axis_c_edges_methods.false_merge_check: The existing Trajectory node has one producer (run_md, classical Velocity-Verlet). PIMD is a second producing method with quantum estimators attached, analogous to how ThermalConductivity carries transport_model tags. Add as a producer variant / edge, not a new node.

axis_c_edges_methods.physics: Adaptive Gaussian bias along collective variables -> free-energy surface (sum_hills). The producing operator for the new FreeEnergySurface node.
axis_c_edges_methods.false_merge_check: No existing free-energy-along-CV producer. New edge into the new FreeEnergySurface node.

recipes: implied by the free-energy tier; not a standalone cookbook recipe but the standard alternative to metadynamics
axis_c_edges_methods.physics: Alternative route to free-energy differences by integrating dH/dlambda across a coupling parameter. A second producer for the FreeEnergy-tier node.
axis_c_edges_methods.false_merge_check: Same target as metadynamics (free energy), different method. A producer variant.

axis_c_edges_methods.physics: Fourier transform of a dipole (or polarizability) autocorrelation along an MD trajectory -> IR (or Raman) spectrum. A producer chain: Trajectory -> DipoleMoment(t) -> autocorrelation -> IRAbsorptionSpectrum. Mirrors the existing HeatCurrent -> HeatCurrentACF -> Green-Kubo kappa chain.
axis_c_edges_methods.false_merge_check: Structurally identical to the existing Green-Kubo autocorrelation pattern but for a different current (dipole vs heat). New operators, reusing the ACF/Fourier machinery.

axis_c_edges_methods.method: NEB / saddle-search (eOn) as a second ReactionBarrier producer
axis_c_edges_methods.physics: Climbing-image NEB, dimer, OCI-NEB producing the minimum-energy-path barrier and saddle geometry. A second producer for the existing ReactionBarrier[construction=neb_mep] node (currently likely grounded on one engine).
axis_c_edges_methods.false_merge_check: The node ReactionBarrier[construction=neb_mep] already exists. eOn is an additional producing rail, not a new node. Confirms the NEB deferred hook.

axis_c_edges_methods.method: Ring-polymer instanton as a rate producer
axis_c_edges_methods.physics: Semiclassical instanton optimization on the ring-polymer surface -> tunneling-corrected rate constant. Producer for the new TunnelingRate node.
axis_c_edges_methods.false_merge_check: New target (rate constant), new producer. No merge.

top10_ranked_candidates.physics: Path-integral MD samples the quantum partition function via ring polymers; unlocks all nuclear-quantum-effect observables.
top10_ranked_candidates.why: One rail opens an entire missing physics layer (NQE) and grounds Trajectory + quantum HeatCapacity; very broad field use.

top10_ranked_candidates.physics: F(s) = -kT ln P(s) along a collective variable, from metadynamics.
top10_ranked_candidates.why: The single biggest missing physics tier; function-valued, fits the existing spectrum contract; central to all of chemistry/biophysics/materials sampling.

top10_ranked_candidates.physics: Adaptive-bias free-energy sampling and collective variables.
top10_ranked_candidates.why: The producer for candidate 2; ubiquitous across the MD field; pairs 1:1 with the FreeEnergy tier.

top10_ranked_candidates.physics: Electronic g(E), Fermi level, gap on an energy grid.
top10_ranked_candidates.why: Fits the spectrum contract; parent of the existing BandGap; high demand. Guard against the PhononDOS false-merge.

top10_ranked_candidates.physics: Climbing-image NEB and dimer saddle search for reaction barriers.
top10_ranked_candidates.why: Directly confirms + strengthens the existing ReactionBarrier[neb_mep] node with a second producer; broad kinetics use.

top10_ranked_candidates.physics: n(w)alpha(w) from the dipole power spectrum (fluctuation-dissipation).
top10_ranked_candidates.why: Confirms the deferred spectrum-layer hook; reuses the existing ACF/Green-Kubo machinery; classic observable.

top10_ranked_candidates.candidate: QuantumHeatCapacity as a HeatCapacity producer variant
top10_ranked_candidates.physics: PIMD scaled-coordinates estimator of C_V for anharmonic/liquid systems.
top10_ranked_candidates.why: Grounds the existing HeatCapacity node with a method-tagged quantum estimator; small increment on top of the i-PI rail.
Second first-principles engine grounding existing DFT nodes; broad use; low new-node cost (nodes already exist).
Distinct from the bulk DielectricTensor; opens the molecular optical-response corner; modest scope.

top10_ranked_candidates.physics: Per-atom shielding tensor and isotropic shift (ppm), with uncertainty.
top10_ranked_candidates.why: Entirely new spectroscopy corner with strong experimental linkage (structure determination); no merge risk. Lower rank because it sits far from the map's current lattice-dynamics core.

deferred_hooks_confirmed.note: Which of the map's known deferred hooks the cookbook independently corroborates as real demand.

confirmed.by: eon-pet-neb recipe; the ReactionBarrier[construction=neb_mep] node already exists and eOn is a second producer (climbing-image NEB + dimer).
water-ir-spectrum (IR absorption), pet-mad-dos (electronic DOS) - both function-valued spectra beyond PhononDOS, confirming the deferred spectrum-layer generalization.
water-pulsed (dielectric response, dipole orientation), polarizability (molecular alpha), water-ir-spectrum (dynamical/Born charges) - the deferred polarization hook has clear cookbook demand.

partial - heat-capacity (quantum C_V of water) and MolecularFrequency-adjacent recipes touch molecular thermodynamics, though the cookbook does it via PIMD rather than rigid-rotor-harmonic-oscillator.

path-integrals, heat-capacity, pi-metad - a whole recipe cluster on nuclear quantum effects; the map's IsotopeAbundances leaf and any isotope-fractionation hook is strongly corroborated as a real theme.

not_independently_confirmed_by_cookbook.note: The cookbook runs plenty of MD (water-md, water-model) where RDF is standard analysis, but no recipe page surfaced RDF as a headline product. Weaker independent confirmation than expected; still a natural MD-analysis node.
No diffraction recipe in the cookbook. Not confirmed here (it is an ML-potential/sampling collection, not a characterization one).
No dedicated defect-formation recipe surfaced. Not confirmed by this source.
No Seebeck/ZT recipe; the cookbook's transport recipe is phonon (lattice) thermal conductivity, already covered by kALDo. Not confirmed.

open_questions: License confirmation needed for i-PI (repo mixes MIT/GPL components) and eOn before any rail is minted (the credits rule blocks an unlicensed rail).

Whether QuantumHeatCapacity and quantum-KE become method-tagged siblings of existing nodes (like ThermalConductivity[transport_model=...]) or standalone nodes - a kernel/tiering decision, deferred to the map-kernel work.
Whether CollectiveVariable is an evidence node or a representation-only axis descriptor for FreeEnergySurface (leaning axis-descriptor).

ModelUncertainty / committee disagreement: recommended as a record-level 'uncertainty' attribute (already in the instance schema) rather than a node; needs a project decision before any UQ recipe is encoded.

ElectronDensity / ChargeDensity is field-valued (3D grid) and exceeds the current scalar+spectrum evidence contract; confirms the QE scan's deferred charge-density node but needs a field-evidence kernel first.
metatomic as the standard MLIP delivery interface: when PET-family potentials are encoded, decide whether metatomic gets its own credit line or rides on the underlying engine's rail (ASE/LAMMPS).

Was the water-ir-spectrum IR node's value-record axis (absorption vs frequency) expressible under the current spectrum canonical-axis registry, or does it need a new registered axis+value unit pair (absorption coefficient)?
